title: Half Adder Logic Gate Implementation
subject: Digital System Design (DSD)
textbook_reference: M. Morris Mano, Digital Design, Pearson, Chapter 4, Page 125

recent_questions: Design a Half Adder circuit using basic logic gates (Dec 2023).
Implement a Full Adder using two Half Adders and an OR gate (May 2023).

theory: A Half Adder is a combinational arithmetic circuit that performs the addition of two single-bit binary numbers, producing a Sum bit (S) and a Carry bit (C). It has two inputs, A and B, and two outputs, S and C. The Boolean expression for Sum is the exclusive-OR of the inputs (S = A XOR B), representing the modulo-2 addition. The Boolean expression for Carry is the logical-AND of the inputs (C = A AND B), representing the overflow bit. A half adder cannot handle a carry-in bit from a previous stage of addition, which is why a Full Adder is required for multi-bit additions.
key_points: Performs single-bit binary addition of two inputs.
Sum (S) expression: S = A ⊕ B (implemented using a 2-input XOR gate).
Carry (C) expression: C = A · B (implemented using a 2-input AND gate).
Cannot accommodate a carry bit from a preceding stage.
Forms the building block of full adders and binary multipliers.
use_cases: Constructing full adders and ripple carry adders inside ALUs.
Simple bitwise addition and parity generator circuits.

title: Full Adder Logic Gate Implementation
subject: Digital System Design (DSD)
textbook_reference: M. Morris Mano, Digital Design, Pearson, Chapter 4, Page 128

recent_questions: Design a Full Adder circuit using basic logic gates and explain its truth table (May 2024).
Implement a Full Adder using 3-to-8 line Decoder and active-low OR gates (Dec 2023).

theory: A Full Adder is a combinational logic circuit that performs the addition of three binary inputs: two significant bits, A and B, and a Carry-in bit (Cin) from a previous lower-order addition stage. It generates two outputs: a Sum (S) and a Carry-out (Cout). The Boolean expression for Sum is the three-input exclusive-OR (S = A XOR B XOR Cin). The Boolean expression for Carry-out is Cout = A·B + B·Cin + Cin·A, representing the majority vote of the inputs. Full adders are cascaded to perform binary addition of multi-bit numbers.
key_points: Performs addition of three bits (two inputs + one carry-in).
Sum (S) expression: S = A ⊕ B ⊕ Cin.
Carry-out (Cout) expression: Cout = AB + BCin + ACin.
Can be built using two 2-input XOR gates, two 2-input AND gates, and one OR gate.
Cascaded in parallel (ripple carry) to form n-bit adders.
use_cases: Arithmetic Logic Units (ALUs) for integer operations.
Binary multipliers and address calculation units inside CPUs.
